hook: ChatGPT just gave you a 9-paragraph essay. You asked for a quick answer. Again.
caption:
ChatGPT just gave you a 9-paragraph essay. You asked for a quick answer. Again.

This isn't an AI problem. It's a calibration problem.

AI defaults to impressive. Long, structured, thorough — because that's what gets rated highly across millions of users.

But you're not millions of users. You're you.

Maybe you think in bullet points. Maybe you hate bullet points. Maybe you need the answer first and the reasoning after. Maybe you need the reasoning first or you don't trust the answer.

None of that is weird. It's just how your brain works.

The frustrating part? You already KNOW how you like information delivered. You've known since third grade. AI just... doesn't.

So you spend 10 minutes re-prompting. Asking it to be shorter. Simpler. More direct. Less formal. "Actually, can you just—"

And next conversation? Back to square one.

That's the gap bootfile.ai was built for. Your thinking style, your communication preferences, your context — packaged into a profile that travels with you into every AI conversation.

No more training it from scratch. No more getting content meant for everyone.

Just AI that actually sounds like it knows you.
